name: MeCodeGen

values.name: fromContext
values.comment: produce code from Dict of name -> expression

values.name: toString
values.comment: turn Expr into Elm code (w/pretty printing)

name: MeFloat
comment:
helper module for Float types

@docs init, toFloat

values.name: toFloat
values.comment: convert Expr to raw Float (if types match)

name: MeInt

values.name: init
values.comment: turn raw Int into an Expr

values.name: toInt
values.comment: convert Expr to raw Int (if types match)

name: MeList

values.name: initFloats
values.comment: convert list of floats to an Expr

values.name: initInts
values.comment: convert list of ints to an Expr

values.name: sortBy
values.comment:
wraps List.sortBy for general types
(but needs an unboxer like `MeInt.toInt`)

Often you can just use `sortByInt` or `sortByFloat` for convenience.

values.name: toList
values.comment:
convert wrapped list to List

You must pass in a converter for the elements. Example:

        expr
            |> MeRunTime.getFinalValue
            |> toList MeInt.toInt
            |> Result.withDefault []
values.type: (MeType.Expr -> Result.Result String.String a) -> MeType.V -> Result.Result String.String (List.List a)

values.name: toListInts
values.comment: convert wrapped list of ints to List Int

name: MeNumber
comment:
wrappers for numbers

@docs plus, minus, mult

name: MeRunTime
comment:
The MetaElm RunTime (MeRunTime) can evaluate
AST expressions inside Elm!


# main API

@docs computeExpr, compute, getFinalValue


# helpers

Helpers are mostly used for wrapping library functions
like List.map.

@docs getFuncV, getFuncVV, getValue, error

values.name: compute
values.comment: like computeExpr, but you can pass in a context

values.name: getFinalValue
values.comment:
get the value of a computed expression (usually
after a call to `compute`)

values.name: getFuncV
values.comment: get an expression that wraps a function taking one argument

values.name: getFuncVV
values.comment: get an expression that wraps a function taking two arguments

values.name: getValue
values.comment:
convert expression to value (or error if it's
a function

name: MeType
